21.correct: The word 'Kathak' derives from 'katha' (story); it literally refers to the act of storytelling, as Kathak dancers narrate tales through movement and expression.

wrong.A: 'Dance of rhythm' is not the literal meaning of Kathak.
wrong.D: 'Rhythmic cycle' (tala) is a musical concept, not the meaning of Kathak.

21.fact: Kathak is a North Indian classical dance that evolved in temples and Mughal courts.

22.correct: The Mahabharata mentions that dying during Uttarayana (the sun's northward journey) leads to moksha - Bhishma waited for Uttarayana to give up his life.

wrong.A: The Ramayana does not contain this Uttarayana-moksha idea prominently.
wrong.C: The Bhagavata Purana is not the source of this specific belief.
wrong.D: The Vedas are hymns/rituals, not the source of this narrative.

22.fact: Bhishma had the boon of 'ichha-mrityu' (death at will) and chose to die after Uttarayana began.

23.correct: 88 Earth days x 86,400 seconds/day = about 7.6 x 10^6 seconds, so the orbital period is roughly 7.6 x 10^6 s.

wrong.B: 6.8 x 10^6 s is too small for 88 days.
wrong.C: 7.1 x 10^6 s does not match 88 days.
wrong.D: 7.9 x 10^6 s is slightly too large.

23.fact: A planet with an 88-day orbit is Mercury, the closest planet to the Sun.

24.correct: Jupiter is called the 'storm planet' because of its enormous, long-lived storms, most famously the Great Red Spot.

wrong.A: Mercury has no thick atmosphere to sustain storms.
wrong.C: Mars has dust storms but is not the 'storm planet'.
wrong.D: Venus has thick clouds but is known as the hottest planet, not the storm planet.

24.fact: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm larger than the Earth that has raged for centuries.

25.correct: The second BRIC Summit (2010) was held in Brazil (Brasilia).

wrong.A: Russia (Yekaterinburg) hosted the first BRIC summit in 2009.

25.fact: The grouping was 'BRIC' until South Africa joined to make it 'BRICS' from 2010-11.

26.correct: Per the announcement, the theme of India's 79th Independence Day celebrations at the Red Fort in 2025 reflected the idea of a 'Naya Bharat' (New India).

wrong.A: 'Azadi ka Amrit' was the earlier Amrit Mahotsav theme.
wrong.C: 'Vikas ki Manzil' was not the chosen theme.

26.fact: India celebrated its 79th Independence Day on 15 August 2025.

27.correct: Evaporation from the oceans is the major source of water vapour in the atmosphere, since oceans cover most of the Earth's surface.

wrong.B: Combustion releases some water vapour but is minor compared to ocean evaporation.
wrong.C: Photosynthesis consumes water rather than being a major vapour source.
wrong.D: Industrial smoke is a minor, localised source.

27.fact: Water vapour is the most abundant greenhouse gas and drives the water cycle.

28.correct: Narinder Singh Kapany, an Indian-American physicist, is called the 'Father of Fibre Optics' for his pioneering work on transmitting light through glass fibres.

wrong.A: Kunal Kapoor is not associated with fibre optics.
wrong.B: Vivek Sahgal is not the fibre-optics pioneer.
wrong.D: Jay Chaudhry is a cybersecurity entrepreneur, not the fibre-optics pioneer.

28.fact: Kapany coined the term 'fibre optics' in the 1950s.

29.correct: The Luni is the main river that flows through the desert of Rajasthan before draining into the Rann of Kutch.

wrong.A: The Chambal flows through south-eastern Rajasthan, not the desert.
wrong.B: The Banas is a tributary of the Chambal, not the desert's main river.
wrong.D: The Sabarmati flows mainly through Gujarat.

29.fact: The Luni's water turns saline in its lower course as it crosses the arid region.

30.correct: Loktak Lake, the largest freshwater lake in north-east India, is located in Manipur and is famous for its floating 'phumdis'.
30.fact: Keibul Lamjao, the world's only floating national park, lies on Loktak Lake and protects the endangered Sangai deer.

31.correct: Zakat was a religious tax levied on all Muslims (a form of obligatory charity) during the Delhi Sultanate.

wrong.A: 'Mamluk' refers to slave soldiers/the slave dynasty, not a tax.
wrong.D: 'Ushr' was a land tax on Muslims' produce, but the religious tax on all Muslims here is zakat.

31.fact: Non-Muslims under the Sultanate paid the jizya tax instead.

32.correct: The Union Home and Cooperation Minister unveiled the National Cooperation Policy 2025 in New Delhi in July 2025.

wrong.A: It was not unveiled in January.
wrong.C: It was not unveiled in April.
wrong.D: It was not unveiled in May.

32.fact: The Ministry of Cooperation was created in 2021 with the motto 'Sahkar se Samriddhi' (prosperity through cooperation).

33.correct: For the month of July 2025, Gujarat was ranked first in the implementation of the PM-JANMAN scheme.

wrong.A: Madhya Pradesh was not ranked first that month.

33.fact: PM-JANMAN targets the development of Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs).

34.correct: The electric unmanned helicopter 'Sabal 20', inducted by the Indian Army in November 2024, was developed by IIT Kanpur.

wrong.A: IIT Bombay did not develop Sabal 20.
wrong.B: IIT Delhi was not the developer.
wrong.D: IIT Madras was not the developer.

34.fact: Such unmanned helicopters can carry supplies to forward or high-altitude posts.

35.correct: As announced in January 2025, the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) developed an indigenously produced 'paracetamol' (with a domestic route for its key raw material).

wrong.B: AIIMS is a medical institute, not the developer.
wrong.C: Indira Gandhi Medical College is not credited with this.
wrong.D: Jubilee Mission Medical College is not the developer.

35.fact: Reducing import dependence for drug raw materials (APIs) is a key goal of India's bulk-drug policy.

36.correct: Tokyo was the host city of the Summer Olympic Games in 2020 (held in 2021 due to the pandemic).
36.fact: Tokyo also hosted the Summer Olympics in 1964.

37.correct: The 4x600 m (II) is NOT a standard relay event, whereas the 4x400 m (I) is a recognised relay - so 'Only II' is the event that is not a relay.

wrong.A: The 4x400 m IS a standard relay, so 'Only I' is wrong.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because the 4x400 m is a genuine relay.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong because the 4x600 m is not a standard relay event.

37.fact: Common track relays are the 4x100 m and 4x400 m.

38.correct: By Fleming's left-hand rule, the second finger (the forefinger is the field) - i.e. the middle/second finger - represents the direction of the electric current.

wrong.B: The forefinger (first finger) represents the magnetic field.
wrong.C: The thumb represents the force/motion on the conductor.
wrong.D: Fleming's rule does not represent resistance.

38.fact: Fleming's left-hand rule is used for motors; the right-hand rule is used for generators.

39.correct: 'Swaramelakalanidhi', an important treatise on music (melas/ragas), was composed by Ramamatya.
39.fact: Ramamatya's 16th-century work was an early systematic classification of South Indian ragas.

40.correct: Magnetite is the best variety of iron ore, containing about 70% iron (the highest iron content among iron ores).

wrong.A: 25% is far too low for magnetite.

40.fact: Hematite is the most widely used iron ore, while magnetite has the highest iron content.
